dimensions_in_order.evidence_excerpt: <str:Dimension id="COUNTRY" ... position="0"; ACCOUNTING_ENTRY position="1"; INDICATOR position="2"; SECTOR position="3"; COUNTERPART_SECTOR position="4"; COUNTERPART_COUNTRY position="5"; FREQUENCY position="6"; <str:TimeDimension id="TIME_PERIOD">. Enumeration Refs in DSD children: COUNTRY/COUNTERPART_COUNTRY -> IMF.STA:CL_PIP_COUNTRY(2.0.0); ACCOUNTING_ENTRY -> IMF:CL_ACCOUNTING_ENTRY(2.2.2); INDICATOR -> IMF.STA:CL_PIP_INDICATOR(4.0.0); SECTOR & COUNTERPART_SECTOR -> IMF:CL_SECTOR(2.13.1); FREQUENCY -> IMF:CL_FREQ(1.2.0).

codelist_members_used.COUNTRY / COUNTERPART_COUNTRY: ISO-3 alpha codes (e.g. USA, JPN, GBR, DEU, CHN, FRA all PRESENT). NOTE: 2-letter codes like 'US' are ABSENT. Codelist also holds special aggregates e.g. GX031='World Minus 25 Significant Financial Centers', TX983='Not Specified (including Confidential)', TX091='International Organizations'. Source: https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/codelist/IMF.STA/CL_PIP_COUNTRY/2.0.0
codelist_members_used.INDICATOR: 60 codes; P_TOTINV_P_USD=Total investment Positions; P_F51_P_USD=Equity; P_F3_P_USD=Debt securities (with _S short / _L long splits); plus DIC_<ccy> currency-of-denomination variants. Source: https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/codelist/IMF.STA/CL_PIP_INDICATOR/4.0.0
codelist_members_used.SECTOR / COUNTERPART_SECTOR: S1=Total economy (the 'total' member; NOT _T), S12=Financial corporations, S121=Central bank, S1Q=Other sectors than MFIs, S1SP=Total economy incl. SPEs. Source: https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/codelist/IMF/CL_SECTOR/2.13.1

keys_tried.key: US.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD.... (reporter='US', the original contract example key)
keys_tried.diagnosis: ROOT CAUSE of DP2's '0 observations'. HTTP 200 but empty: the COUNTRY codelist CL_PIP_COUNTRY uses ISO-3 codes (USA), not the 2-letter 'US'. The SDMX API silently returns a valid-but-empty dataset for a syntactically-valid-but-nonexistent member rather than erroring, so the wrong country code looks like 'no data'. The wild middle dimensions (....) were fine; only the reporter code was wrong.

keys_tried.sample_obs: series COUNTRY=USA ACCOUNTING_ENTRY=A INDICATOR=P_TOTINV_P_USD SECTOR=S1 COUNTERPART_SECTOR=S1 COUNTERPART_COUNTRY=ABW FREQUENCY=A SCALE=6 -> Obs TIME_PERIOD=2024 OBS_VALUE=170000000 (USD; ~$170m, US holdings of Aruba securities)
keys_tried.diagnosis: WORKS. 13064 bilateral series / 26128 obs returned (3.86 MB) for a single reporter, spanning many COUNTERPART_COUNTRY codes. Confirms the only fault in the old key was the 2-letter reporter.

keys_tried.key: USA.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD.S1.S1.JPN.S (fully specified minimal bilateral cell: US holder, Assets, total PI, holder sector S1=Total economy, issuer sector S1, counterpart=JPN, semi-annual)
keys_tried.diagnosis: WORKS. Clean single-cell bilateral series. SECTOR total member is S1 (Total economy), not _T. Semi-annual code is S. Latest available period = 2025-S1 (end-June 2025).

keys_tried.sample_obs: series COUNTRY=USA ... COUNTERPART_COUNTRY=JPN FREQUENCY=A -> TIME_PERIOD=2023 OBS_VALUE=1217880000000 (USD)
keys_tried.diagnosis: WORKS. 154 series across sector cross-classifications confirms the holder x issuer sector cross-tabulation (from-whom-to-whom by sector) is populated, not just sector totals.

evidence_for_verdict: Working bilateral key: GET https://api.imf.org/external/sdmx/2.1/data/IMF.STA,PIP,5.0.0/USA.A.P_TOTINV_P_USD.S1.S1.JPN.S (accept: application/vnd.sdmx.structurespecificdata+xml;version=2.1). Real bilateral observation: creditor=USA, debtor/counterpart=JPN, instrument=total portfolio investment assets, period=2025-S1 (end-June 2025), OBS_VALUE=1344145000000 USD (~$1.344 trillion of US-resident holdings of Japanese-issued portfolio securities); full semiannual series 2023-S1..2025-S1 returned. Bilateral cells are dense across counterpart economies (26128 obs for a single reporter) and across the holder-sector x issuer-sector cross-classification (154 series for USxJPN alone).
implication: Yes, it flips DP2's tag. CPIS/PIP bilateral data is fully OBTAINABLE; the '0 observations / HOLE / one-dense-column-only' verdict was an artifact of a wrong reporter code (2-letter 'US' instead of ISO-3 'USA' in CL_PIP_COUNTRY), which the API returns as HTTP 200 with an empty dataset rather than an error.
